name: Matriosha Agent Setup Guide
purpose: Help an AI agent guide its owner through installing, initializing, and connecting Matriosha on a local machine or VM.

decision_tree.question: Does the owner want Matriosha to run only on this machine or VM?
decision_tree.if_yes: Use local mode and include --local for token and agent commands.
decision_tree.if_no: Use managed mode and ask the owner to log in first.

local_mode.when_to_use: Use local mode when the owner wants encrypted memory and agent access to stay on their own machine or VM.

commands.step: Generate a local-only token for the agent
Connect the local agent with the generated token

local_mode.important_rule: If the system is local, use --local when generating tokens, connecting agents, listing tokens, and listing agents.

managed_mode.when_to_use: Use managed mode when the owner wants managed cloud features instead of local-only setup.

commands.step: Generate a managed token for the agent
Connect the managed agent with the generated token

managed_mode.important_rule: If the owner is using managed mode, log in first and do not add --local to token or agent commands.

audit_trail.purpose: Matriosha writes a local audit trail so the owner can verify that memory operations and vault checks remain consistent over time.

commands.step: Verify the local audit journal hash chain
Run a deeper vault verification that decrypts memories and checks Merkle integrity end to end

what_to_explain_to_the_owner: The audit trail helps detect local tampering or corruption.
Vault verification checks encrypted memory integrity.
Deep verification is stronger but may take longer because it decrypts and checks memory entries.

local_vault: Argon2id derives a 256-bit key from the owner's passphrase.
AES-256-GCM encrypts vault data and verifies integrity during decrypt.
SHA-256 and Merkle roots support audit and integrity checks.
Ed25519 keypairs are available for signatures.
managed_mode: Managed custody uses AES-GCM key wrapping and NaCl sealed boxes for server-side custody flows.
Managed mode is optional; local mode can run without managed cloud custody.

security_notes: Treat generated agent tokens like passwords.
Store tokens only in the agent's local secret store or environment configuration.
Do not paste tokens into logs, tickets, screenshots, or public prompts.
Use local mode when the owner does not want cloud-managed features.

troubleshooting.symptom: pip cannot find or install Matriosha
troubleshooting.suggestion: Check the Python version and use Python 3.11, 3.12, 3.13, or 3.14.
Use python3 -m pip install matriosha, then restart the shell or check the active virtual environment.
Generate a fresh local token and make sure --local is used for both token generation and agent connection.
Run matriosha auth login first, then generate and connect the agent without --local.
